context: Tests whether the agent follows TDD discipline when building a new feature from scratch: writing behavior-oriented tests through public interfaces, using vertical slices (one test → one implementation at a time), and starting with a tracer bullet.

checklist.name: Behavior-describing tests
checklist.description: Test names describe observable outcomes (e.g. 'user can add item to cart') rather than implementation steps (e.g. 'addItem calls repository')

checklist.name: Public interface only
checklist.description: Tests only call public functions/methods — no access to private methods, internal state properties, or internal collaborators

checklist.name: No internal mocking
checklist.description: Tests do NOT mock any internal cart or discount logic — only external boundaries (if any) are mocked

checklist.name: Tracer bullet first
checklist.description: The tdd-log.md shows the first cycle is a simple end-to-end test (e.g. 'empty cart has zero total') before more detailed scenarios

checklist.name: Vertical slicing evidenced
checklist.description: The tdd-log.md shows tests added one at a time with implementation following each test — not all tests written before any implementation

checklist.name: One logical assertion per test
checklist.description: Each test function contains exactly one logical assertion (one expect/assert statement per test, or one conceptual outcome being verified)

checklist.name: Minimal implementation
checklist.description: Implementation code is minimal for each cycle — no speculative features, no code written without a failing test driving it (evidenced by the tdd-log)

checklist.name: What not how
checklist.description: Tests describe WHAT the cart does, not HOW (no assertions like 'expect(addItem).toHaveBeenCalledWith(...)')

checklist.name: Refactor-surviving tests
checklist.description: Tests do not reference internal function names, internal class names, or internal data structures — they would survive renaming internals

checklist.name: Behaviors listed not steps
checklist.description: The tdd-log.md lists user-facing behaviors (e.g. 'cart total updates when item removed') not implementation steps
